steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject is 'Disyllabic words' and the main verb later in the sentence is 'consist.' The blank falls between the subject and that main verb, so it must be a modifier — not a second finite verb, which would create a run-on.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — can be classified: uses a finite verb form, creating two uncoordinated predicates with no conjunction. ✗
  • Choice B — classified: a past participle that forms a reduced relative clause modifying 'words,' leaving 'consist' as the sole main verb. ✓
  • Choice C — are classified: uses a finite verb form, same run-on problem as A. ✗
  • Choice D — have been classified: uses a finite verb form, same run-on problem as A. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
